Operation 'searchJobs' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary is too generic and doesn't indicate the API source or key search parameters. Original description lacks specificity about what parameters are accepted, what fields are returned in the response, authentication requirements, and sorting behavior. Enhanced version provides AI agents with clear understanding of: (1) the operation's domain context (ZipRecruiter marketplace), (2) expected input parameters (keywords, location, category, experience level), (3) pagination support, (4) specific output schema (JobSearchResponse with Job objects), (5) authentication requirement, and (6) default sorting behavior. This enables better agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Search for job listings on ZipRecruiter matching the specified criteria. Returns paginated results with job details.
data.summary_suggestion: Search ZipRecruiter job listings by criteria
data.description_suggestion: Searches ZipRecruiter's job marketplace for listings matching specified criteria such as keywords, location, job category, and experience level. Supports pagination for browsing large result sets. Returns a JobSearchResponse containing an array of Job objects with details including title, company, location, salary range, and job description. Requires a valid publisher API key for authentication. Results are sorted by relevance by default.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't indicate the API source or key search parameters. Original description lacks specificity about what parameters are accepted, what fields are returned in the response, authentication requirements, and sorting behavior. Enhanced version provides AI agents with clear understanding of: (1) the operation's domain context (ZipRecruiter marketplace), (2) expected input parameters (keywords, location, category, experience level), (3) pagination support, (4) specific output schema (JobSearchResponse with Job objects), (5) authentication requirement, and (6) default sorting behavior. This enables better agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.
